title: ADVNTR Road Intel Pack
description: Canonical weekly artifact. Authored by Stage A, validated and rendered by Stage B. This is the ONLY thing Stage A produces — prose lives inside it as fields. Never author a separate web version and newsletter version.

edition_date.description: The Monday of the edition week. Drives filename, URL path, and utm_campaign.

trailhead.description: The executive summary. Written LAST, after stories are ranked. It is an argument about the week, not a list of what follows.

body.description: 120-220 words. Names the through-line of the week. If there is no through-line, say so plainly rather than inventing one.

thin_week.description: Set true when fewer than 5 stories cleared verification. Renderer adjusts layout and copy; it does NOT trigger padding.

stories.description: PUBLISHED stories only — everything that cleared verification. Harvest was wider; see watch_list for what did not clear.

watch_list.description: single-source or unverified candidates. Never rendered publicly. Carried forward and rechecked next week.

proposed_tags.description: Places or topics Stage A encountered that are not in the registry. PROPOSAL ONLY — Stage B logs these for human review and does NOT mint them. A story whose only place is unregistered is tagged national-park-system or dropped.

harvest_stats.description: Transparency on the harvest-wide / publish-narrow funnel. Rendered internally only.

id.description: Stable slug. Used for cross-edition dedupe. Never reused for a different story.

tier.description: Drives CTA weighting (lead=3, feature=2, brief=1) and which surfaces render `context`. Exactly one story per edition may be `lead`.

headline.description: What happened. Original wording — never the source outlet's headline verbatim.

take.description: REQUIRED ON EVERY TIER. <=40 words. What it means for someone who is going there. If it would be equally true of any park in the system, it is not a take and must be rewritten. See intel-run-prompt.md section 5 for the ban list.

context.description: 80-150 words. REQUIRED for tier lead and feature. MUST BE ABSENT for tier brief. Original prose; no quotation beyond a few words.

confidence.description: confirmed = traces to a primary source AND date verified. reported = 2+ independent secondary outlets, no primary yet; renderer applies visible hedging. single-source never reaches this array — it goes to watch_list.

primary_source.description: REQUIRED. Every published story must anchor to a primary source, not merely to coverage of one. For confidence=reported this may be the closest official page (e.g. a park alerts index) with primary_confirmed=false.

published_date.description: Publication date of the underlying event/announcement. Stage B HARD-FILTERS anything outside [window_start, window_end]. Do not guess — if unverifiable, the story goes to watch_list.

places.description: Registry slugs only. Stage B fails validation on unknown values. Order matters — first is primary.

topics.description: From the closed topics vocabulary in intel-place-registry.yaml.

action.description: What the reader does about it. The sortable axis competitors do not have.

severity.description: Drives CTA suppression when combined with topic=safety.

sensitivity.description: CTA suppression flags. Stage A MUST set these honestly — a CTA attached to a fatality story is unrecoverable brand damage.

impact.description: Drives the guide-update queue. content-stale and fee-changed generate a task; monitor does not.

affects_guides.description: Derived by Stage A from places[] where guide_status is published or in-progress. This is the living-edition mechanism.

effective_dates.description: When the thing actually takes effect, if different from published_date. Powers the longitudinal archive.
